text: Last class you said: I used to have high blood pressure.
Today you can say: Could I have a window seat, please?

Good afternoon. May I see your passport and your booking, please?
Here you are. Could I have a window seat, please? I am flying to a conference in Lisbon.
Of course. You have seat fourteen A, a window seat. Do you have a bag to check in today?
Yes, one suitcase to check in. Can I take this small bag as a carry-on? I keep my laptop in it.
That is fine, as long as it fits in the overhead bin. Here is your boarding pass. Boarding is delayed by twenty minutes.
Thank you. Where can I find gate twenty-six, and could you tell me where security is?
My connecting flight is delayed. Could you help me, please?

Good afternoon, passengers. This is a boarding announcement for flight four seven two to Lisbon. We are now boarding at gate twenty-six. Please have your boarding pass and passport ready for the agent. Passengers with a connecting flight should check the screens for their next gate after they land. Only one carry-on bag is allowed in the cabin, and it must fit in the overhead bin. We are sorry to say that boarding is delayed by twenty minutes because of a late aircraft. We will start boarding business class and families with small children first. Thank you for your patience, and have a pleasant flight.

I travel abroad two or three times a year for medical conferences, so the airport is like a second home to me. When I arrive, the first thing I do is check in and drop off my big suitcase. I always keep my laptop and my notes in my carry-on, because I do not want to lose them. After check-in, I go through security, and then I look for my gate. If I have time, I find a quiet place near the gate to read. I have learned that a few polite phrases make everything easier. I always say, could you help me, please? or where can I find the lounge? People are happy to help when you ask nicely. Travel is much less stressful when you stay calm and ask good questions.

Here is what I do every time I fly. First, I check in and show my passport at the desk. Then I drop off my suitcase and keep my carry-on with me. After that, I go through security with my boarding pass ready. Next, I find my gate and check the screen for any delay. Finally, I wait near the gate until they call my row to board.
